title: HATEOAS Response Bodies with Embedded Caps and next_actions

story.as_a: new agent encountering loopctl for the first time
story.i_want_to: have every successful custody response include the exact next API call I can make (with any minted capability token embedded in the example body), and every error response include a machine-parseable reason code + remediation URL + sample request
story.so_that: I can walk from first contact to verified work using only the response bodies as guidance, without reading source code or external documentation

rationale: Self-discovery (section 8 of chain-of-custody-v2.md) requires responses to teach agents what to do next. Without HATEOAS, an agent holding a fresh capability token has no way to know where to use it. This story adds next_actions to successful responses and structured remediation to error responses — wiring together US-26.3.1 (cap minting), US-26.0.4 (discovery), and US-26.0.5 (system articles) into a complete self-organizing workflow.

acceptance_criteria.description: Every successful custody response (claim, start, report, request_review, review_complete, verify, reject) includes a `next_actions` array of objects: `{action (string), method (HTTP verb), path (URL template), required_body (JSON example with placeholders), preconditions (array of strings), learn_more (URL)}`.
When a response includes a minted capability token, the `next_actions[i].required_body` field shows the exact cap value inlined — so the agent can copy the response body as its next request body.

Error responses for all capability- and lineage-related failures include `error.remediation: {description, next_action, learn_more, example}`. Error codes are stable and documented: `self_verify_blocked`, `missing_capability`, `wrong_lineage`, `expired_capability`, `consumed_nonce`, `replay`, `verifier_needed`.

Each error code has a dedicated system article at slug matching the code (e.g., `/wiki/self-verify-blocked`, `/wiki/missing-capability`). System articles are seeded with placeholder content by this story; full content is authored in US-26.3.3.

A state machine spec file `lib/loopctl/progress/state_machine.ex` declares the legal transitions from each state. The `next_actions` field in responses is auto-generated from this spec, so it cannot drift from the real legal transitions. Adding a new transition in the spec automatically updates all HATEOAS responses.

An integration test walks a naive agent through the full flow: discover → contract → claim → start → implement (no-op for the test) → request_review → verify (via a second lineage) — using ONLY the response bodies for guidance. No hardcoded paths in the test beyond `/.well-known/loopctl`.
The existing loopctl JSON views are updated to include `next_actions` without breaking backward-compatible clients. Clients ignoring the field continue to work.
Error responses include a stable `error.code` field (enum) separate from `error.message` (human-readable). Machine clients should key on `error.code`, not message text.

OpenAPI spec updates: `NextAction` schema, `ErrorRemediation` schema, all error response schemas include the new shape. CHANGELOG entry describing the HATEOAS addition and the stable error code contract.

test_cases.name: Successful claim response includes next_actions with embedded start_cap
self_verify_blocked error includes remediation
steps: Attempt verify with same lineage as implementer
expected_results: error.remediation.next_action mentions requesting a new dispatch

test_cases.name: Naive-agent walk: only response bodies guide the flow
steps: Call each next_action in sequence using only the response bodies
expected_results: Agent reaches verified state without any hardcoded URLs beyond /.well-known/loopctl

technical_notes: State machine spec: Elixir module with a @transitions attribute listing tuples of (current_state, action, next_state, required_cap_typ).
JSON view refactor: a shared helper `LoopctlWeb.Hateoas.add_next_actions/2` that takes a struct and a conn, reads the state machine, and injects next_actions.
Error serialization: update FallbackController.call to wrap errors in the new shape.
The stable error codes should be documented in docs/error-codes.md and cross-linked from CLAUDE.md.
